jokes.text: You know it, Lady Broseph!
jokes.setup: Standard workplace interaction where intern delivers research
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mashup of formal address 'Lady' with fratty 'Broseph'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy with an unexpected gender-bending portmanteau that establishes these characters as tone-deaf ex-bankers

jokes.text: Aren't they a little old, and over-dressed?
jokes.setup: Being told these are new interns
jokes.punchline: Visual contradiction - interns are usually young and casual
jokes.explanation: Solid observational setup that pays off when we learn they're ex-bankers

jokes.text: They're former investment bankers who were laid off after that economic crash that Nancy Pelosi caused.
jokes.punchline: Absurd scapegoating of Nancy Pelosi for the 2008 financial crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - deflecting corporate responsibility while being completely wrong about basic facts

jokes.text: They've got zero real-world skills, but god, they work hard.
jokes.punchline: The contradiction between having no skills but working hard
jokes.explanation: Efficient commentary on Wall Street work ethic vs practical competence

jokes.text: Soap and spanish magazines? What, are you expecting a visit from the...
jokes.setup: Intern about to make what's clearly a racist stereotype
jokes.punchline: The setup to an offensive punchline that gets cut off
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing the intern's tone-deafness, with the cutoff creating cringe comedy

jokes.text: Don't start unless you've got something.
jokes.setup: Intern beginning what's obviously a racist joke
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual shutdown implying he's heard this before
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - suggests Jack regularly deals with racist jokes from these guys while maintaining professional detachment

jokes.text: You should go there. They'd love you.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how Puerto Rican elderly women are respected
jokes.punchline: Liz sarcastically suggesting Jack would fit right in
jokes.explanation: Standard Liz snark - economical but not particularly original

jokes.text: Women should not deliver the mail.
jokes.setup: Liz sorting through mail that isn't hers
jokes.punchline: Randomly sexist declaration that comes out of nowhere
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Liz moment - projecting her own guilt about mail tampering into random misogyny

jokes.text: He's a pediatrician, so you know he likes kids. Or feet. No, kids.
jokes.setup: Liz deducing positive things about Dr. Baird from his mail
jokes.punchline: Brief dark turn into foot fetish territory before correcting
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection structure with excellent economy - the quick correction makes it funnier

jokes.text: And he has a golf magazine, so you know he's not gay or poor.
jokes.setup: Continuing to profile Dr. Baird from his mail
jokes.punchline: Casually offensive stereotyping presented as logical deduction
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Liz's flawed reasoning and casual prejudices

jokes.text: That's a federal offense! And you already have 2 strikes.
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting they look at Dr. Baird's movie preferences
jokes.punchline: Kenneth revealing Liz is a repeat mail offender
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing this is a pattern for Liz, Kenneth as moral authority

jokes.text: Muppets Take Manhattan. Caddyshack. And a documentary about how pies are made.
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly wholesome and specific combination
jokes.explanation: Specific, endearing details that make Dr. Baird seem perfect but quirky

jokes.text: Only the special tours get to see Conan without his wig.
jokes.punchline: Absurd behind-the-scenes 'fact' about Conan O'Brien
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke playing on NBC network synergy while being completely absurd

jokes.text: I've had white boyfriends. Black, Cuban... Brazilian guys with German heritage...
jokes.setup: Jack wondering if grandmother hates him because he's white
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific ethnic categories showing it's not about race
jokes.explanation: Good escalating specificity, though the joke structure is fairly standard

jokes.text: I get it. You've been with other men. Congratulations. Thank you.
jokes.punchline: Jack's awkward attempt to move past the uncomfortable oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack awkwardness - trying to be supportive while clearly uncomfortable

jokes.text: He looks exactly like me.
jokes.setup: Jack seeing El Generalissimo on the telenovela
jokes.punchline: The visual reveal that creates the episode's central premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's main plot - simple but effective visual comedy premise

jokes.text: He does look like you.
jokes.setup: Jack's realization about resembling the villain
jokes.punchline: Elisa's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Simple confirmation beat that sells the visual gag

jokes.text: This honky grandma be trippin'.
jokes.setup: White ex-banker trying to bond with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at AAVE that's completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing the banker's cultural tone-deafness

jokes.text: When I quote lines from your movie, that's not racist? Of course not. I transcend race.
jokes.setup: Intern worried about appropriateness of quoting Tracy
jokes.punchline: Tracy's grandiose claim about transcending race
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy ego moment - completely missing the point while being pompous

jokes.text: You shouldn't end a sentence with a preposition at.
jokes.punchline: Tracy 'correcting' grammar while making it worse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant grammar joke that demonstrates the rule by breaking it - perfect Tracy pseudo-intellectualism

jokes.text: A yard, like a lawn? Yes, I have.
jokes.setup: Intern asking if Tracy has drunk a yard of beer
jokes.punchline: Tracy misunderstanding but claiming he's done it anyway
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy logic - completely missing the concept but claiming he's done it

jokes.text: He has an ice cream maker? Come on!
jokes.setup: Liz discovering more about Dr. Baird
jokes.punchline: Ice cream maker as evidence he's too perfect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's insecurity about deserving someone good

jokes.text: Don't be weird-looking.
jokes.setup: Liz about to meet Dr. Baird
jokes.punchline: Impossible self-instruction
jokes.explanation: Good Liz anxiety moment - trying to control something uncontrollable

jokes.text: I live in the basement, so I have all the nails.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Oswald is helping hang pictures
jokes.punchline: Bizarre logic about basement living and nail ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd logic delivered deadpan - great weird character moment

jokes.text: Sorry, I smell like frosting. I just love to bake.
jokes.punchline: Another perfect quality that makes him even more appealing
jokes.explanation: Functional character building - making Dr. Baird impossibly perfect

jokes.text: I want to go to there.
jokes.punchline: Liz's childlike grammar breakdown from attraction
jokes.explanation: Iconic Liz catchphrase moment - grammatically broken but emotionally perfect

jokes.text: Boy, I hope those two don't eventually have a showdown that pits family against justice.
jokes.setup: Elisa mentioning cousin's police academy graduation and other cousin's parole party
jokes.punchline: Jack predicting obvious dramatic storyline
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy about obvious dramatic setups, delivered with Jack's characteristic detachment

jokes.text: As a child, my grandmother worked in a silver mine without proper ventilation. Now, her mind is squishy like a hacky sack.
jokes.setup: Explaining grandmother's confusion about the Generalissimo
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific metaphor for brain damage
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and dark metaphor - 'squishy like a hacky sack' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Even though sometimes she comes at me with a knife because of a silver frenzy.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of violent episodes
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with 'silver frenzy' as a perfectly absurd condition

jokes.text: Told him his haircut was exactly what I've been trying to describe to my barber. Smooth, I know. I'm way out of my league here.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking how Liz got Dr. Baird's picture
jokes.punchline: Liz's awkward but effective pickup line followed by self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Liz's surprising smoothness followed by characteristic insecurity

jokes.text: He gets all of the movie channels, including Starz.
jokes.setup: Reading more of Dr. Baird's mail
jokes.punchline: Starz presented as the ultimate luxury
jokes.explanation: Solid comedy about premium cable hierarchies and what impresses Liz

jokes.text: And... he trains seeing eye dogs at home.
jokes.setup: Continuing to list Dr. Baird's perfect qualities
jokes.punchline: The most impossibly perfect quality yet
jokes.explanation: Good escalation making Dr. Baird comically perfect, sets up the allergy problem

jokes.text: That's no good for you. You're allergic to dogs. Guess I'll have to step in then.
jokes.setup: Learning Dr. Baird trains seeing eye dogs
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's opportunistic offer
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth moment showing his opportunistic side beneath the innocent facade

jokes.text: He pulled an uncle Harland Parcell last night. Minus the wistful jug blowing.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre family reference with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth weirdness - specific family reference with inexplicable detail about jug blowing

jokes.text: Have you ever tasted Scotch? It's terrible! And this thing they call 'box seats at the Rangers game,' it's so cold!
jokes.setup: Tracy describing his night out with the interns
jokes.punchline: Complaining about luxury experiences
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character work - experiencing rich people activities for the first time and hating them

jokes.text: And what is Rohypnol?
jokes.setup: Tracy asking about drugs he encountered
jokes.punchline: Innocent question about date rape drug
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from Tracy's innocence about what he was given

jokes.text: So I shouldn't have taken two of them for my headache?
jokes.punchline: Revealing he took them voluntarily
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - making the situation worse with Tracy's naivety

jokes.text: Tonight, shooters at Blockheads, lay a base, meet up with my bro-hams at Cornell club? You know it, bri-man!
jokes.punchline: Fratty language that Tracy mirrors
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing how Tracy gets sucked into their world

jokes.text: I have no idea what either one of us just said.
jokes.punchline: Tracy's honest confusion about the language he's adopting
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-moment acknowledging the absurdity while staying in character

jokes.text: Here come the roofies. You can do whatever you want to me.
jokes.setup: Tracy feeling the effects of the drugs
jokes.punchline: Dangerous vulnerability delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Dark callback that pays off the earlier setup effectively

jokes.text: What? Come on, you're a nurse? Some of us are hot.
jokes.setup: Jack introducing Elisa as a nurse
jokes.punchline: Liz's inappropriate comment about nurse attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's tendency to say the wrong thing

jokes.text: What? Come on, you're a nurse? Some of us are hot.
jokes.punchline: Immediate stereotype contradiction and narcissism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna combination of stereotype busting and self-absorption

jokes.text: Boy, that guy looks a lot like you!
jokes.punchline: Liz stating the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that moves the plot forward

jokes.text: He just blew up that kid!
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about child murder
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy moment establishing the villain's extremes

jokes.text: her adorably broken english. Metrocards are a real thing. You use them on the subway.
jokes.setup: Jack listing obstacles in their relationship
jokes.punchline: Example of 'broken English' that's actually correct information
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Jack's condescension while Elisa provides factual information

jokes.text: Opened her mail? That is just shocking. He's a monster.
jokes.setup: Learning the Generalissimo opened someone's love letters
jokes.punchline: Liz's outrage while being guilty of the same thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback - Liz condemning exactly what she's been doing all episode

jokes.text: Or he has a really good reason to do it.
jokes.setup: Just calling the Generalissimo a monster
jokes.punchline: Immediately justifying the same behavior
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Liz's instant rationalization of her own behavior

jokes.text: My dog ran away! Let me get my coat.
jokes.setup: Learning how the Generalissimo manipulates women
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately applying the same technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Liz becoming the Generalissimo - perfect economy and character integration

jokes.text: If we don't, I don't think I could ever be around dogs again.
jokes.setup: Fake dog search with someone who trains seeing eye dogs
jokes.punchline: Liz unknowingly creating a problem for their potential relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Liz sabotaging herself while manipulating him

jokes.text: I forgot I even had this on. I have so many charity t-shirts.
jokes.punchline: Casual abundance of charitable involvement
jokes.explanation: Continues the character trait with casual humblebragging

jokes.text: I'm actually on the board of the Pediatric Restless Leg Syndrome Association. I forgot I even had this on. I have so many charity t-shirts.
jokes.punchline: Comically specific charity work and humble bragging about charity shirts
jokes.explanation: Good absurd specificity - 'Pediatric Restless Leg Syndrome' is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Shoot it, dog! I'm going to! I'm Tracy Jordan. Why would I be afraid of fire next to my mouth?
jokes.setup: Tracy doing something dangerous while still roofied
jokes.punchline: Irrational confidence based on celebrity status
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - celebrity status as protection from physics

jokes.text: Wait, wait. What did he just say? That wasn't in the script.
jokes.punchline: Realizing the actor has gone off-script
jokes.explanation: Good plot advancement showing Jack losing control of the situation

jokes.text: And now, this potion's gonna make me live forever.
jokes.punchline: Instead claims immortality
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation that completely derails Jack's plan

jokes.text: Morena has gone broken arrow.
jokes.setup: Actor going rogue on the show
jokes.punchline: Military terminology for entertainment crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment applying corporate military speak to soap opera drama

jokes.text: We really should have had somebody on the set who speaks Spanish.
jokes.punchline: Obvious oversight stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good corporate incompetence moment delivered with perfect Jack understatement

jokes.text: Do not try to out-generalissimo me, my friend.
jokes.punchline: Using character name as a verb
jokes.explanation: Great character work - actor so invested in role he turns the name into a verb

jokes.text: I get to cut the line at Disneyland, if I come mid-week.
jokes.setup: Hector listing benefits of his role
jokes.punchline: Pathetically specific perk with major limitation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific and pathetic perk - the mid-week limitation makes it hilarious

jokes.text: I have a lucrative endorsement deal with Sabor de Soledad!
jokes.punchline: Product name meaning 'Flavor of Loneliness'
jokes.explanation: Great fake product name that's both Spanish-authentic and hilariously depressing

jokes.text: Play ball, or you'll be back doing R-rated hypnotism at bull fights so fast, you won't know what hit you.
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific past career
jokes.explanation: 'R-rated hypnotism at bull fights' is brilliantly specific and absurd - perfect threat

jokes.text: You can lose your mind!
jokes.setup: Jack and Hector bonding over similarities
jokes.punchline: Singing together
jokes.explanation: Good bonding moment showing their unexpected connection

jokes.text: I am super gay, and I would totally switch for her.
jokes.punchline: Extreme compliment about her attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing just how attractive Elisa is through extreme reaction

jokes.text: Seduction is never impossible for El Generalissimo.
jokes.setup: Jack saying seducing grandmother is impossible
jokes.punchline: Hector's confidence in his character's abilities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hector's total investment in the role

jokes.text: Like Julie Harris in The Belle of Amherst. You are surprisingly gay.
jokes.punchline: Obscure theater reference followed by Jack's observation
jokes.explanation: Great character detail with obscure theater reference plus perfect Jack deadpan

jokes.text: What did you not understand? That picture is from a dog food ad.
jokes.punchline: Revealing the dog search was always fake
jokes.explanation: Great revelation that recontextualizes the entire subplot

jokes.text: I translated it, and took out the Star Wars references.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of removing inappropriate references
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about writer tendencies and cultural translation

jokes.text: Good. Don't listen to anyone who says you should become an actress.
jokes.punchline: Liz's blunt career advice
jokes.explanation: Good Liz moment - practical advice delivered bluntly

jokes.text: He drugged her champagne and had his way with her.
jokes.punchline: Casual description of assault
jokes.explanation: Sets up the parallel to Liz's plan but the execution is fairly standard

jokes.text: Later, she gave birth to the devil. You know, sweeps week.
jokes.punchline: Casual TV industry reference to explain supernatural plot
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-comedy explaining absurd soap opera logic with industry terminology

jokes.text: That's what I could do to Drew.
jokes.punchline: Liz deciding to replicate sexual assault tactics
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing how far Liz will go, perfectly in character

jokes.text: But you haven't seen Dr. Baird. He looks like a cartoon pilot.
jokes.punchline: Liz's specific and bizarre description of attractiveness
jokes.explanation: 'Cartoon pilot' is a perfectly weird and specific way to describe attractiveness

jokes.text: Be very careful. El Generalissimo was once a good man. But the years of loneliness drove him insane.
jokes.punchline: Soap opera backstory as life advice
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment using soap opera as cautionary tale for real life

jokes.text: I am not the Generalissimo.
jokes.setup: Being warned about becoming evil through deception
jokes.punchline: Liz's denial while actively planning deception
jokes.explanation: Good ironic denial that the audience knows isn't true

jokes.text: It's like I said in my not-hit comedy Cruise Boat, 'I'm getting too old for this ship.'
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about keeping up with young people
jokes.punchline: Terrible movie pun on 'Lethal Weapon' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - bad movie title with worse pun, acknowledging his own failure

jokes.text: Do you know what happens to a comedian when he gets old and loses his audience? He starts to get offered serious roles.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he needs to stay young
jokes.punchline: Serious acting as career death sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta commentary on comedy careers and Hollywood with perfect Tracy logic

jokes.text: And do you really want to see me play Arthur Ashe?
jokes.setup: Explaining why he needs to avoid serious roles
jokes.punchline: Specific, inappropriate casting example
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd casting idea that would indeed be terrible

jokes.text: The party's tomorrow night. Tonight, I'm just hanging out, eating fondue by candlelight.
jokes.setup: Drew arriving for the fake party
jokes.punchline: Liz's obviously staged romantic scene
jokes.explanation: Good execution of the Generalissimo plan with obviously fake spontaneity

jokes.text: I guess one glass couldn't hurt.
jokes.punchline: Exactly what Liz predicted he'd say
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Liz's manipulation working exactly as planned

jokes.text: I found your dog! He lost his collar, but I recognize him from the poster.
jokes.punchline: Someone actually finding a dog for the fake search
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that shows how lies spiral out of control

jokes.text: How could he not want to come back to this beautiful lady?
jokes.punchline: Sweet comment while unknowingly enabling deception
jokes.explanation: Shows Drew's kindness while highlighting the deception

jokes.text: Buster, you wang!
jokes.punchline: Calling a dog 'wang'
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny insult for a dog, shows Liz's frustration

jokes.text: Let's get you back in your special kitchen cabinet.
jokes.punchline: Dog lives in kitchen cabinet
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail about where stuffed dog is stored

jokes.text: You've been roofied!
jokes.punchline: Realizing Drew took Tracy's pills
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting the two plots

jokes.text: That's not your dog. I can explain. Just relax.
jokes.setup: Drew discovering opened mail and fake dog
jokes.punchline: Multiple deceptions being revealed simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as all of Liz's lies unravel at once

jokes.text: I am the Generalissimo!
jokes.punchline: Embracing the villain identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character culmination - Liz fully becoming what she denied being

jokes.text: I am the Generalissimo! I don't know what that means.
jokes.punchline: Drew's confused response to telenovela reference
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment with Drew's practical confusion

jokes.text: I don't know what that means.
jokes.punchline: Confused response deflating the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with innocent confusion

jokes.text: I don't know what that means.
jokes.punchline: Drew's confused normalcy
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Liz's dramatic moment with practical confusion

jokes.text: The word 'whom' deserves a defender.
jokes.punchline: Noble mission to defend archaic pronoun usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Drew character detail - earnest about grammar in charmingly nerdy way

jokes.text: Monthy Python, Live at the Hollywood Bowl. Classic.
jokes.punchline: Appreciating her taste in comedy
jokes.explanation: Shows they have compatible interests despite the deception

jokes.text: I'm a flip-flopper.
jokes.setup: Having memberships to meat and vegetarian clubs
jokes.punchline: Political term applied to dietary choices
jokes.explanation: Good use of political terminology for personal inconsistency

jokes.text: The word 'whom' deserves a defender.
jokes.punchline: Passionate defense of formal grammar
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail showing Drew's pedantic but endearing qualities

jokes.text: weirder things have happened, right? Isn't that Tracy Jordan?
jokes.setup: Trying to rationalize their weird meet-cute
jokes.punchline: TV news showing something even weirder
jokes.explanation: Good transition to the Tracy subplot resolution

jokes.text: I'm doing this so no one will know I'm getting old. But you just told us you're old. This interview is over.
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining his own strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic failure - defeating his own purpose in explaining it
